# @rokkit/data

Tabular data manipulation for JavaScript. Provides a fluent `dataset()` pipeline for grouping,
alignment, rollup aggregation, filling missing values, and set joins — plus standalone join
functions and a declarative filter engine.

## Import

```js
import { dataset, crossJoin, leftJoin, filterData } from '@rokkit/data'
```

## dataset() — fluent pipeline

`dataset(rows)` wraps an array of plain objects and returns a chainable `DataSet`. Call `.select()`
at the end to execute the pipeline and get the result array back.

```js
const result = dataset(rows)
  .where(row => row.active)
  .sortBy('name')
  .select()
```

### Filtering and sorting

```js
// .where(predicate) — keep rows matching a function
dataset(orders).where(o => o.amount > 100).select()

// .sortBy(...keys) — sort ascending by one or more keys
dataset(products).sortBy('category', 'price').select()
```

### Column operations

```js
// .rename({ oldKey: 'newKey' }) — rename columns
dataset(rows).rename({ amt: 'amount', qty: 'quantity' }).select()

// .drop(...keys) — remove columns
dataset(rows).drop('internal_id', 'temp').select()

// .apply(fn) — transform each row
dataset(rows).apply(row => ({ ...row, total: row.price * row.qty })).select()

// .fillNA({ field: defaultValue }) — fill null/undefined values
dataset(rows).fillNA({ revenue: 0, label: 'N/A' }).select()
```

### Row operations

```js
// .update(value | fn) — update rows matching the current .where() filter
dataset(rows).where(r => r.status === 'pending').update({ status: 'active' }).select()

// .remove() — delete rows matching the current .where() filter
dataset(rows).where(r => r.deleted).remove().select()
```

### Set operations

```js
// .union(other) — combine two datasets (append rows)
dataset(a).union(b).select()

// .minus(other) — rows in a that are not in b (deep equality)
dataset(a).minus(b.select()).select()

// .intersect(other) — rows in a that are also in b
dataset(a).intersect(b.select()).select()
```

### Joins (chainable)

```js
// .innerJoin / .leftJoin / .rightJoin / .fullJoin / .semiJoin / .antiJoin
// condition is a function (rowA, rowB) => boolean

dataset(orders).leftJoin(dataset(customers), (o, c) => o.customerId === c.id).select()

// .crossJoin — all combinations of two datasets
dataset(sizes).crossJoin(dataset(colors)).select()

// .nestedJoin — nest matching rows under a key (default 'children')
dataset(departments).nestedJoin(dataset(employees), (d, e) => d.id === e.deptId).select()
```

### Aggregation — groupBy + rollup

`groupBy` + optional `alignBy` + optional `summarize` + `rollup` is the aggregation pipeline.

```js
// Group and collect children (default aggregator — produces nested rows)
const nested = dataset(sales)
  .groupBy('region')
  .rollup()
  .select()
// → [{ region: 'West', children: [{ month: 'Jan', revenue: 4200 }, ...] }]

// Group and aggregate with a summary formula
import { mean, sum } from 'd3-array'

const byRegion = dataset(sales)
  .groupBy('region')
  .summarize(row => row.revenue, { revenue: sum })
  .rollup()
  .select()
// → [{ region: 'West', revenue: 142000 }, ...]

// Group by multiple keys
dataset(sales)
  .groupBy('region', 'product')
  .summarize(row => row.revenue, { revenue: mean })
  .rollup()
  .select()
```

### alignBy — fill missing combinations

`alignBy` ensures that every group has a row for every value of the aligned field that exists
anywhere in the dataset. Missing rows are filled using `usingTemplate`.

Use case: animation frames — ensure every (x, color) group has a data point for every year,
filling gaps with zero so bars animate smoothly rather than jumping in and out.

```js
// Pre-aggregated data: one row per (class, drv, year)
// Some (class, drv) groups may be missing certain years.
const complete = dataset(aggregated)
  .groupBy('class', 'drv')     // one group per (class, drv) combination
  .alignBy('year')              // ensure all years appear in every group
  .usingTemplate({ hwy: 0 })   // fill missing year rows with hwy = 0
  .rollup()
  .select()
// → [{ class: 'compact', drv: 'f', children: [{ year: 1999, hwy: 29 }, { year: 2008, hwy: 31 }] },
//    { class: 'suv',     drv: '4', children: [{ year: 1999, hwy: 0 }, { year: 2008, hwy: 15 }] }]

// Flatten children back to flat rows:
const flat = complete.flatMap(row =>
  row.children.map(({ actual_flag: _, ...child }) => ({
    class: row.class, drv: row.drv, ...child
  }))
)
```

**`actual_flag`**: `alignBy` adds an `actual_flag` field to all child rows (1 = real data, 0 = filled).
Strip it after flattening if not needed.

## Standalone join functions

The join functions are also exported as plain functions that operate on raw arrays:

```js
import { innerJoin, leftJoin, rightJoin, fullJoin, crossJoin, antiJoin, semiJoin } from '@rokkit/data'

// leftJoin(first, second, condition) — all rows from first, matched rows from second
const result = leftJoin(users, roles, (u, r) => u.roleId === r.id)

// crossJoin(first, second) — all combinations
const grid = crossJoin(rows, cols)

// antiJoin(first, second, condition) — rows from first with NO match in second
const unmatched = antiJoin(all, matched, (a, b) => a.id === b.id)

// semiJoin(first, second, condition) — rows from first WITH a match in second (no duplication)
const matched = semiJoin(orders, activeCustomers, (o, c) => o.customerId === c.id)
```

## filterData — declarative filtering

```js
import { filterData, filterObjectArray } from '@rokkit/data'

// Apply multiple filter rules in sequence
const rules = [
  { column: 'status', operator: '=', value: 'active' },
  { column: 'revenue', operator: '>', value: 1000 }
]
const filtered = filterData(rows, rules)

// Single filter on one column
const one = filterObjectArray(rows, { column: 'region', operator: '=', value: 'West' })

// Filter across all columns (omit column)
const search = filterObjectArray(rows, { operator: '~*', value: /rokkit/i })
```

Operators: `=`, `!=`, `<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`, `~` (regex), `~*` (regex), `!~`, `!~*`

## Common use cases

### Pre-aggregate chart data

```js
import { dataset } from '@rokkit/data'
import { mean } from 'd3-array'

// One mean value per (category, year) for a bar chart
const chartData = dataset(rawRows)
  .groupBy('category', 'year')
  .summarize(row => row.sales, { sales: mean })
  .rollup()
  .select()
```

### Complete animation frames

```js
// After aggregating: ensure all frame values appear for every group
const completeData = dataset(aggregated)
  .groupBy('category')
  .alignBy('year')
  .usingTemplate({ sales: 0 })
  .rollup()
  .select()
  .flatMap(row =>
    row.children.map(({ actual_flag: _, ...child }) => ({ category: row.category, ...child }))
  )
```

### Enrich one dataset with fields from another

```js
const enriched = leftJoin(
  products,
  categories,
  (p, c) => p.categoryId === c.id
)
```

### Find rows missing from a reference set

```js
const newItems = antiJoin(incoming, existing, (a, b) => a.id === b.id)
```

### Build a complete combination grid

```js
// All (size, color) combinations for a product configurator
const variants = crossJoin(sizes, colors)
```

## ReactiveDataSet — Svelte 5 reactive wrapper

File: `@rokkit/data/src/dataset.svelte.js` (must be imported directly — rune compilation required)

`reactiveDataset(rows)` holds source data in `$state` and exposes a `rows` getter backed by
`$derived`. Templates bind to `ds.rows` directly; the result auto-recomputes when source data or
configuration changes.

```js
import { reactiveDataset } from '@rokkit/data/src/dataset.svelte.js'

const ds = reactiveDataset(rawRows)
ds.where(r => r.status === 'active').sortBy('name')

// Template binding (Svelte 5)
// {#each ds.rows as row}...{/each}

// CRUD — triggers reactive update automatically
ds.push({ id: 99, name: 'New', status: 'active' })
ds.remove(r => r.id === 5)
ds.update(r => r.status === 'pending', { status: 'active' })
ds.setData(freshRows)
```

### ReactiveDataSet API

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `rows` (getter) | Reactive derived result — bind to this in templates |
| `.where(fn)` | Set filter predicate (replaces any previous filter) |
| `.apply(fn)` | Append a row transform |
| `.sortBy(...fields)` | Set sort fields (replaces previous sort) |
| `.clearFilter()` | Remove the current filter |
| `.clearTransforms()` | Remove all transforms |
| `.setData(rows)` | Replace entire source dataset |
| `.push(row)` | Append a row to the source |
| `.remove(pred)` | Remove rows matching predicate from source |
| `.update(pred, patch)` | Update matching rows (object or function patch) |
| `.snapshot()` | Return a plain non-reactive copy of current `rows` |

### When to use DataSet vs ReactiveDataSet

- **`dataset()`** — for one-shot data preparation outside components (pre-aggregation, chart prep)
- **`reactiveDataset()`** — for in-component reactive data (search/filter UIs, CRUD lists)
